pages.name: Documentation Home
pages.description: Landing page for the AI evaluation platform documentation.
primary_functions: Provide an overview of the documentation structure and core capabilities
Offer quick links to Getting Started, API Reference, Concepts, Guides, Examples, Playground, and SDKs
Expose a global search input to search across all docs content
Allow switching the visible API version (e.g., v1, v2) via a version selector in the header

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the "Getting started" header link or the Getting Started card on the home page
Click the "API Reference" header link or API Reference overview card
Click the "Concepts" header link or Concepts overview card
Click the "Guides" header link or Guides overview card
Click the "Examples" header link or Examples gallery card
Click the "Playground" header link or Playground card
Click the "JavaScript SDK" header link or SDKs card
Submit the global search form in the header
Click the "About" footer link
Click the "Support" footer link

access_methods.description: Primary entry point via the "Home" header link or site logo
Accessible via footer link back to documentation home

pages.name: Getting Started
pages.description: Introductory section for new users to set up and run their first evaluations.
primary_functions: Explain key prerequisites and basic setup steps for using the evaluation platform
Provide a sidebar list of getting started topics including the Text Classification Quickstart
Route users to specific quickstart pages such as the Python text classification quickstart

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the "Quickstart: Text classification" item in the Getting Started sidebar or content list
Click breadcrumb or logo to return to Documentation Home

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "Getting started" header navigation link
Linked from overview sections and cards on the Documentation Home page

pages.name: Quickstart: Text Classification
pages.description: Step-by-step quickstart for creating a text classification evaluation.
primary_functions: Present language tabs (e.g., Python, JavaScript) with code samples for text classification evaluations
Provide a 'Run your first evaluation' section with an editable Python code block including metrics, max_samples, and model configuration
Allow users to adjust metrics (e.g., accuracy, f1), max_samples (e.g., 200), and model id (e.g., gpt-4.1-mini) directly in the code block
Enable copying the fully configured evaluation script using a copy button on the code block

incoming_params.description: Optional language code (e.g., 'python', 'javascript') to preselect a language tab on load

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "Next: API Reference" or similar link after completing the quickstart

access_methods.description: Accessed via links from the Getting Started page and search results
Optional 'lang' query parameter selects the initial language tab

pages.name: API Reference Overview
pages.description: Central hub for REST API reference documentation.
primary_functions: List core API resource groups (e.g., Evaluations, Datasets) in a sidebar navigation
Describe authentication, base URLs, common headers, pagination, and error handling concepts for the APIs
Provide links from the sidebar to specific endpoint pages such as Create Batch Evaluation and Create Dataset
Reflect the selected API version (e.g., v1 or v2) in all linked endpoint docs

incoming_params.description: Optional API group identifier (e.g., 'evaluations', 'datasets') to pre-expand a sidebar group

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the "Create batch evaluation" endpoint within the Evaluations group in the API sidebar
Click the "Create dataset" endpoint within the Datasets group in the API sidebar
Click breadcrumb or logo to return to Documentation Home

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "API Reference" header navigation link
Linked from the Documentation Home page overview sections

pages.name: API: Create Batch Evaluation
pages.description: Detailed reference for the batch evaluation creation endpoint.
primary_functions: Document the REST endpoint path, HTTP method, path parameters (e.g., project_id), and query parameters (e.g., limit, cursor)
Show example requests with language tabs including a curl example for creating batch evaluations
Allow users to edit the example URL inline to replace placeholder project IDs (e.g., {project_id} -> proj_123) and append query strings (e.g., ?limit=50&cursor=cursor_abc)
Provide a copy button for the complete curl command, including any JSON body and updated URL

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the API Reference breadcrumb or "All endpoints" link in the sidebar

access_methods.description: Accessed from the API Reference sidebar under the Evaluations group

pages.name: API: Create Dataset
pages.description: Detailed reference for the dataset creation endpoint.
primary_functions: Document the REST endpoint path for creating datasets (e.g., /v1/datasets or /v2/projects/{project_id}/datasets depending on version)
Describe required and optional fields in the dataset creation request body
Provide example requests with language tabs including a curl example for the POST request
Enable users to copy the curl example that targets the appropriate v2 dataset creation endpoint after switching the docs version selector

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the API Reference breadcrumb or "Back to Datasets" link

access_methods.description: Accessed from the API Reference sidebar under the Datasets group

pages.name: Concepts Overview
pages.description: High-level conceptual documentation for the evaluation platform.
primary_functions: Introduce core concepts such as evaluations, datasets, metrics, and models
Provide a concepts sidebar linking to deeper topics such as Metrics Overview
Help users choose which conceptual topics to read before using the APIs or SDKs

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the "Metrics" or "Metrics overview" link in the Concepts sidebar or topic list

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "Concepts" header navigation link
Linked from the Documentation Home page overview sections

pages.name: Metrics Overview
pages.description: Overview of all available metrics in the platform.
primary_functions: List available metrics (e.g., accuracy, f1, latency, BLEU, cost) with short descriptions and categorization
Highlight metrics related to billing, including a cost metric whose description mentions token-based billing
Link each metric entry to a Metric Detail page for deeper configuration and usage examples

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a specific metric (e.g., the cost metric mentioning token-based billing) in the metrics list

access_methods.description: Accessed from Concepts overview and other internal links discussing metrics

pages.name: Metric Detail
pages.description: Detailed documentation for a single metric such as the token-based cost metric.
primary_functions: Explain the purpose, calculation, and recommended use cases for the selected metric
Provide a JSON configuration example block with fields like price_per_1k_tokens, currency, and unit for configurable metrics such as cost
Allow inline editing of the JSON example to set values (e.g., price_per_1k_tokens to 0.002, currency to "USD", unit to "tokens")
Offer a copy button (e.g., 'Copy JSON') to copy the edited metric configuration

incoming_params.description: Metric identifier (e.g., 'cost') whose detail and configuration should be displayed

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "Back to metrics" link or breadcrumb

access_methods.description: Accessed via 'metricId' query parameter when a metric is selected from Metrics Overview or search

pages.name: Guides Overview
pages.description: Collection of task-oriented guides for configuring and running evaluations.
primary_functions: List available guides (e.g., Config Builder, Best Practices, Advanced Metrics) with summaries
Provide a guides sidebar that highlights and links to the Config Builder guide
Help users choose which practical guide to follow based on their goals

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the "Config builder" or "Configuration builder" guide in the Guides list or sidebar

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "Guides" header navigation link
Linked from the Documentation Home overview sections

pages.name: Guide: Config Builder
pages.description: Interactive guide for building evaluation configuration JSON or code.
primary_functions: Provide a 'Task type' dropdown to select evaluation types (e.g., Text generation) for configuration
Offer a 'Models' or 'Variants' section to add and edit model rows, including model IDs and temperature settings for candidate and baseline models
Include an 'Evaluation settings' panel to set parameters such as max_samples and other global options
Provide a metrics selection area with checkboxes or toggles to enable metrics such as accuracy and latency while disabling others
Generate evaluation configuration JSON or code when users click 'Generate config', ready for copying or export

outgoing_connections.trigger: Follow a link like "Use this config with the API" after generating a configuration

access_methods.description: Accessed from the Guides overview sidebar as the highlighted Config Builder guide

pages.name: JavaScript SDK
pages.description: Reference and examples for using the JavaScript SDK.
primary_functions: Describe installation, initialization, and common patterns when using the JavaScript SDK
Present SDK-specific tabs or sections with JavaScript-focused examples including batch evaluations
Provide a code example that calls runBatchEvaluation with a configuration or options object
Allow users to adjust options such as concurrency (e.g., set concurrency to 10) directly in the example, and copy the modified code using a copy button

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click links from SDK docs to the underlying REST endpoints in the API Reference

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "JavaScript SDK" header navigation link
Accessed via search results and contextual links from other docs

pages.name: Examples Gallery
primary_functions: Display a grid or list of example evaluations with key metadata such as task type, metrics used, and dataset size
Provide filters including a metric dropdown (e.g., BLEU) and a dataset size filter with a minimum value input (e.g., set minimum to 1000)
Offer an 'Apply filters' control to refresh the gallery based on selected metric and dataset size constraints
Allow users to click individual example cards (including the third example in a filtered list) to open the corresponding Example Detail page

incoming_params.description: Optional metric ID (e.g., 'bleu') to preselect in the gallery metric filter
Optional minimum dataset size to initialize the dataset size filter (e.g., 1000)

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on an example card in the filtered examples gallery (e.g., the third BLEU example)

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "Examples" header navigation link
Metric and dataset size filters can be initialized via 'metricId' and 'minSize' query parameters

pages.name: Example Detail
pages.description: Detailed breakdown of a specific evaluation example.
primary_functions: Describe the example's task, dataset, configuration, and metrics (e.g., a BLEU-based evaluation with at least 1000 samples)
Show code snippets or config for reproducing the example in various environments
Include a 'REST API' or 'API endpoint' section that displays the endpoint path used (e.g., starting with /v1/projects/ or /v2/projects/)
Provide a copy icon next to the displayed endpoint path so users can copy the endpoint string directly

incoming_params.description: EvaluationExample identifier used to load the selected example's details and endpoint path

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "Back to examples" link or breadcrumb

access_methods.description: Accessed from the Examples Gallery or search via 'exampleId' query parameter

pages.name: Playground
pages.description: Interactive playground for running sample evaluations directly in the browser.
primary_functions: Allow users to choose a 'Task type' such as Text classification for their playground run
Provide a prompt input area to enter instructions or example text (e.g., 'Classify this review as positive or negative.')
Enable configuring metrics and their weights (e.g., accuracy with weight 0.7 and latency with weight 0.3) while disabling or zeroing other metrics
Offer inputs for sample size (e.g., 10) and evaluation name (e.g., weighted_test_01)
Include a model selector with defaults and a 'Run evaluation' button to execute the configured evaluation

incoming_params.description: Optional task type (e.g., 'text_classification') to preselect the Playground task

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "Playground" header navigation link
Optional 'taskType' query parameter preselects the playground task type

pages.name: Search Results
pages.description: Page showing global documentation search results.
primary_functions: Display a list of search results based on a query entered in the global search input (e.g., runBatchEvaluation)
Label each result by section (e.g., JavaScript SDK, API Reference, Guides) and show a short snippet of matching content
Allow users to click through to relevant pages such as the JavaScript SDK page for results referencing batch evaluations

incoming_params.description: Search query string submitted from the global search input

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a search result whose docPageName corresponds to the JavaScript SDK
Click a search result pointing to the Text Classification Quickstart
Click a search result in the API Reference section
Click a search result related to the Metrics overview
Click a search result leading to the Examples gallery

access_methods.description: Accessed when the global search form navigates here with query parameter 'q'

pages.name: About
pages.description: Background information about the AI evaluation platform and documentation.
primary_functions: Explain the purpose and goals of the evaluation platform and its documentation site
Outline the target audience and supported use cases for the platform
Link to key sections like Getting Started and Support for new users

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a call-to-action link such as "Start with the Quickstart"
Click a link such as "Need help? Visit Support"

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "About" footer navigation link

pages.name: Support
pages.description: Help and troubleshooting resources for users of the evaluation platform.
primary_functions: Provide guidance on how to get help (e.g., email, issue tracker, knowledge base)
Offer troubleshooting tips for common problems with evaluations, APIs, SDKs, and the playground
Link users to relevant documentation sections (e.g., API Reference, Guides, Metrics) for deeper troubleshooting

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click troubleshooting links for API errors
Click troubleshooting links pointing to how-to guides
Click troubleshooting links related to metrics configuration
Click links suggesting trying an evaluation in the Playground

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "Support" footer navigation link

header_links.description: REST API endpoints and reference details
Conceptual overviews including metrics and models
Task-focused guides such as the config builder

footer_links.description: About the AI evaluation platform and docs
List and descriptions of available metrics
